primary_concern: Sepsis risk with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and low platelets (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. AKI progressed from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) within 24 hours. Thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic or hemolytic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine rise >0.3 mg/dL in <48h with Stage 3 classification (≥3x baseline)
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score 3 (score 4 threshold: <20 K/uL)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused workup
Start fluid resuscitation and reassess for vasopressor support
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Assess for AKI reversal strategies (e.g., nephrology consult)
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen integrity. Confirm source of infection and reassess for septic shock criteria (MAP, vasopressor use).

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
